Identiverse 2026 • Session Why Britain Hates Digital ID: A Psycho-Historical Tour Back to Main Agenda Thursday, June 18 Mandalay Bay H 9:40 am - 10:05 am Jaye Hackett Co-founder Vouchsafe An overseas digital identity acquaintance of mine recently posted a harmless LinkedIn update: coming to the UK to speak on a panel, curious what Brits think about digital ID. Pretty gentle, nerdy stuff. A few hours later his comments were a wall of fury; conspiracies of soul-harvesting, globalist plots. The wrath of British LinkedIn had arrived. Britain’s relationship with “papers, please” is uniquely emotional, shaped by centuries of uncomfortable encounters between the state and the individual. Our resistance is full-throated and patriotic, even when it makes no sense at all. In this talk, Jaye takes us on an irreverent but insightful journey through Britain’s ID angst, from the second world war to Tony Blair's ill-fated 2000s foray, and the recent resurrection of its digital ghost. We'll see how the coronavirus pandemic calcified our attitudes into their current dysfunctional form. Understanding this cultural operating system is crucial, because the UK is once again building a national digital ID. And without reckoning with our past, we may doom its future. Expect laughs, uncomfortable truths, and some truly inscrutable LinkedIn comments. stay in touch! Identiverse 2026 • Session From Monthly to Daily: Restoring Trust in Workforce Identity Data When Applications Won’t Integrate Back to Main Agenda Thursday, June 18 Mandalay Bay F 10:15 am - 10:40 am Chris Power Director of IAM Operations Sallie Mae Workforce IAM programs often depend on identity data that is technically “available,” but operationally unreliable. In our environment, more than 100 applications could not be directly integrated with our IGA platform due to missing APIs, brittle interfaces, or turnaround times that made the data unusable for daily identity decisions. As a result, access data was delivered through a patchwork of manual exports, screen scraping, PowerShell scripts, and email or file-share delivery—often only on a monthly basis. This talk shares our multi-year journey to break that pattern and restore trust in workforce identity data without waiting for vendors or application owners to modernize. We’ll walk through how we identified where human interaction was creating risk, defined what “good enough” automation looked like for non-integrated applications, and implemented a repeatable process to convert legacy extracts into timely, validated data suitable for daily ingestion. Attendees will learn how improving data freshness and reliability directly impacted provisioning accuracy, termination handling, and overall IAM operational confidence. The session focuses on practical lessons, tradeoffs, and patterns that other IAM teams can apply when perfect integrations are not an option. stay in touch! Identiverse 2026 • Session Governing Non‑Human Identity for AI Agents in CIAM: Zero Standing Privilege in Practice Back to Main Agenda Thursday, June 18 Mandalay Bay H 10:15 am - 10:40 am Manikandan Rajaram Director, Software Engineering Capital One Anji Yalla Senior Director Capital One Your next privileged user is an agent, one the customer authorizes to act on their behalf. Consumer apps are rapidly adding agentic copilots that can navigate accounts and complete tasks like refunds, address changes, plan upgrades, or money movement. That’s a UX win, and a fraud magnet; prompt injection, malicious browser extensions, and token replay can turn delegated convenience into account takeover and unauthorized transactions across web, mobile, and OBO channels. This session presents a governance model for customer authorized agents using zero standing privilege (ZSP). Agents hold no persistent account access; they receive intent scoped, short lived permissions bound to the runtime (sender constrained / proof‑of‑possession) and to explicit customer consent. We’ll cover patterns for agent registration and attestation, delegated authorization (OAuth/OIDC), step‑up and transaction confirmation for high risk actions, optional wallet/VC signals for higher assurance, continuous risk evaluation using behavioral telemetry, and rapid revocation with audit ready evidence of who authorized what. Attendees will leave with CIAM control objectives, policy patterns, and an implementation checklist to enable secure in-app agents and “bring your own agent” experiences without sacrificing conversion or support outcomes. stay in touch! You’d Think We’d Be Better at This Back to Main Agenda Thursday, June 18 Mandalay Bay K 11 am - 11:30 am Eve Maler President/Founder Venn Factory Andrew Cameron Distinguished Engineer, IAM GM Pamela Dingle Director of Identity Standards Microsoft Ian Glazer Head of Continuous Identity Product Strategy CrowdStrike Our industry isn’t defined by some ancient history but instead is a dynamic and emergent collection of lived experience, trial and error, good ideas, and “what were they thinking” moments. …something people just like you may have experienced and continue to experience. Technical standards, industry organizations, and trends come and go; their history and their future is best understood through the people who participated in them and even caused them to happen. With over 100 years of industry experience on stage, this panel will discuss our shared history, best guesses as to the industry’s direction, and what excites them about the future of our practice. Attendees will: Gain lessons on responding to challenges and opportunities through the shared experiences of the panelists Learn the “why” behind many commonly used standards and practices, whether loved or un- Become emboldened in realizing the future of identity is theirs, individually, to shape stay in touch!
